title: [Bug]: Oracle payload_field idempotency accepts structured values as keys
summary: Oracle payload-field idempotency converts structured payload values to string representations instead of rejecting them.
observed: A selected payload field containing an object or list can become an idempotency key through a language-specific string representation.
expected: payload_field idempotency must resolve to a scalar value before it can be used as a key.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that extracts a payload_field value whose value is an object.
impact: Structured values can produce unstable or surprising idempotency keys.
delivery_semantics: The fix raises a permanent validation error before Oracle commit so the core can avoid ACK and use DLQ policy when configured.
security: Restricting idempotency keys to scalar values keeps durable identity explicit and reviewable.
acceptance: Scalar payload_field values continue to work.
Oracle mapping tests continue to pass.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, Oracle mapping tests, Oracle sink contract tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update Oracle idempotency documentation or release notes if scalar key requirements are clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
